entry.title: Third-Party Payments with SIPO (SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition)
entry.content:
This blog post introduces business scenarios and explains how to process payment with SIPO in Czech Republic for SAP S/4HANA Cloud public edition.  

SIPO (Soustředěné Inkaso Plateb Obyvatelstva) that is used for direct debit of multiple payments through the Czech Post’s system. It is a national payment aggregation system operated by the Czech Post. This business concept relates to payments through a third party. 

Business Scenarios 

Let’s start with the parties involved in SIPO payments.   

  1. Customers that have SIPO connection number pay on a regular basis  
  2. Service providers that offer services of utilities, multimedia, or internet, send payment requests to SIPO  
  3. Czech Post Office, or the SIPO operator, collects all payment requests, aggregates them into one payment, and distribute paid money by customers to service providers  
  4. Banks that serve as payment channels carry out direct debit from bank accounts 

There are two business scenarios of payments involving SIPO:  

Scenario 1: You, as a service provider, post receivable items. After executing a payment run, you send a SIPO file, as an outgoing file, to the Czech Post for informing the customers to pay.   

Scenario 2: Continuing with scenario 1, you, as a service provider, want to clear the open items by processing incoming SIPO files after your customer completes the payment. 

Prerequisites 

Bank Account Settings   

Since the payments with SIPO order are not directly paid out from the payer's bank account, instead it is paid out from the post office, it is necessary to create a fictive bank account and account ID for the post office.   

Open the Manage Bank Accounts app.   

For your company code, create a fictive Bank Account and Account ID for the Czech Post.

Define Parameters for Paying Out    

Since payment is carried out through the third party (Czech Post),  you need to identify which payee whom the post office pays.    

In the Define Parameters for Foreign Payment Transactions (ID: 101953) configuration to fill the service provider's SIPO number in the Customer number field.  

Define Payment Methods  

Open the Define Payment Methods (ID: 101952) to create a payment method or use the pre-delivered format - CZ_PSTLPAYMENT_SIPO as the payment medium format for Czech SIPO payments.

Define Payment Lot Specifications   

Open the Define Payment Lot Specification activity (ID:107255).   

Select the format type Czech Republic: Postal Payment Item (SIPO file) (CZ-POSTAL-PAYMENT-ZA) and complete the Bank statement settings section.   
Note: It is not required to configure the Czech Republic: Postal Payment Header (SIPO file) (CZ-POSTAL-PAYMENT-PZ).

Define Interpretation Rules for Note to Payee    

Open the Define Interpretation Rules for Note to Payee activity (ID: 103949) to assign keywords.

Procedure

Processing SIPO Outgoing Payment Files 

  1. You post contract accounting receivable items.  
  2. Execute a payment run. The payment order then is created.  
  3. During the payment run, the system uses the payment format CZ-POSTAL-PAYMENT-SIPO to generate the payment file for the Czech Post.  
  4. You then send the payment file to Czech Post.  
    Czech Post informs the customer to conduct the payment.  

Processing SIPO Incoming Payment Files

After the customer pays, the Czech Post sends back the SIPO files.   

The SIPO files contain the following files:  

  • SIPO header file – Includes the total settlement amount that matches the bank statement of the incoming payment  
  • SIPO item file – lists the clearing of open receivable items per customer’s individual SIPO number 

You have to import both files in the Manage Incoming Payment File (3rd-Party Payments) app (F1680).   

  1. Open the Manage Incoming Payment File (3rd-Party Payments) app (F1680). 
    • Select Import and choose Third-party Payment.
    • Select Czech Republic: Postal Payment Header (SIPO file) and upload the SIPO header file.  
    • Repeat the same operation and upload the Czech Republic: Postal Payment Item (SIPO file).  
  2. Open the Process Imported Third-Party Payments app (F9000) to schedule a run. 
    • Select Third-Party Payment: Transfer data as the job template.   
    • In the Parameters, select Czech Republic: Postal Payment Item (SIPO file) (CZ-POSTAL-PAYMENT-ZA) as a format type.   
      Note: When processing the imported file, you only need to process the item files. (Header files are not required.)  
    • Schedule a run and you can see that the payment lot number is created in the log .
      If you select the Post Lot check box and you can directly process the payment order lot without additional operation.  
  3. Run the payment lot and you clear the open items. Use the payment lot and process it in the Process Payment (Order) Lot app and clear open items of the customer. If you did not select the checkbox in the previous step. 

Is this blog post useful to you? Leave your comments below to let us know your thoughts.

entry.title: Turn tax into strategy: Margin steering with SAP PaPM and SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Transfer pricing rarely gets the spotlight.  But it should.

In this partner webinar, we open the doors to a real SAP journey.  A shift from fixed license rates to a margin-based model powered by Transactional Net Margin Method (TNMM).  The goal was clear.  Align tax, profitability, and compliance with the realities of a cloud business.

You will see how SAP built an end-to-end solution using SAP Profitability and Performance Management, SAP Analytics Cloud, and Settlement Management.  Not theory.  Actual execution.

The result.  Better audit readiness.  Less manual work.  A model that scales as the business grows.

The thing is, margin steering is not just a finance exercise.  It gives your customers a way to stay within arm’s length ranges while keeping profitability predictable.

If you work with EPM, xP&A, or tax-driven transformations, this session will give you practical ideas you can apply fast.

Register now and see how strategy meets execution.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2026/apr/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00013760.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPEcosystem #SAPAnalyticsCloud #EPM #xP&A #TransferPricing

entry.title: v 2 oData error
entry.content:
Hi,

 

Kindly check the oData error.

sap.ui.define([
    "sap/ui/thirdparty/jquery",
    "sap/ui/core/date/UI5Date",
    "sap/ui/core/util/MockServer",
    "sap/base/Log"
], (jQuery, UI5Date, MockServer, Log) => {
    "use strict";

    return {
        /**
         * Initializes the mock server.
         * You can configure the delay with the URL parameter "serverDelay".
         * The local mock data in this folder is returned instead of the real data for testing.
         * @Public
         */
        init() {
            // create
            const oMockServer = new MockServer({rootUri: "/"});

            oMockServer.simulate("../localService/metadata.xml", {
                sMockdataBaseUrl: "../localService/mockdata",
                bGenerateMissingMockData: true
            });
            // handling mocking a function import call step
            const aRequests = oMockServer.getRequests();
            aRequests.push({
                method: "GET",
                path: new RegExp("FindUpcomingMeetups(.*)"),
                response: (oXhr) => {
                    Log.debug("Incoming request for FindUpcomingMeetups");
                    const oToday = UI5Date.getInstance();
                    oToday.setHours(0); // or oToday.toUTCString(0) due to timezone differences
                    oToday.setMinutes(0);
                    oToday.setSeconds(0);
                    // the mock server only works with sap.jQuery.ajax and async: false. But the request does not
                    // actually go to a server, so this does not block the main thread.
                    jQuery.ajax({
                        url: `/Meetups?$filter=EventDate ge datetime'${oToday.toISOString()}'`,
                        dataType : 'json',
                        async: false,
                        success : (oData) => {
                            oXhr.respondJSON(200, {}, JSON.stringify(oData));
                        }
                    });
                    return true;
                }
            });
            oMockServer.setRequests(aRequests);

            // handling custom URL parameter step
            const fnCustom = (oEvent) => {
                const oXhr = oEvent.getParameter("oXhr");
                if (oXhr?.url.includes("first")) {
                    oEvent.getParameter("oFilteredData").results.splice(3, 100);
                }
            };
            oMockServer.attachAfter("GET", fnCustom, "Meetups");


            // start
            oMockServer.start();

            Log.info("Running the app with mock data");
        }
    };
});

Thanks

Kaushik

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Action Box linking F...
entry.content:
Dear user,

the "Manage Complaints Against Supplier" refers to a new Fiori object that has been introduced (see here) with relevant data of defective materials can be recorded in the new Complaint against Supplier, and subsequently returned to the supplier by automatically creating a returns purchase order from the Complaint. The new Fiori object has no association with the Quality Notification and is to be considered as a modern Fiori-based alternative to using the Quality Notification for the notification type 'Q2'. 

Warm regards
Varun

entry.title: Re: Cheques Encashment date field is missing in FBL1N table
entry.content:
In ECC, the Cheque Encashment Date field was available in classic line item reports like FBL1N because those reports pulled directly from the classic tables (BSIK/BSID/BSEG) and displayed many payment-related fields.

The Cheque Encashment Date field is not part of the standard field catalog in FBL1N in S/4HANA. Instead, payment-related details (including cheque information) are accessed through Payment Information reports or via the Universal Journal (ACDOCA) and related CDS views.

So what you can do is use Fiori apps like Supplier Line Items or Supplier Balances which are based on CDS views. These apps expose payment information differently. If the business requires the Cheque Encashment Date in FBL1N or equivalent, you can extend the CDS view behind the report to include the field from the payment tables.

entry.title: Re: odataVersion 4.01 prevents app from being imported into Work Zone HTML5 Apps
entry.content:
I'm surprised this is not a bigger issue since I just recently used the fiori generator and it seems like OData 4.01 is now the default.

As a test, I tried this with a ui5 app I have connected to an OData 4.01 RAP service. Before deploying the app, I set the manifest.json to 4.0. This obviously caused an error when connecting to the RAP service, "unsupported OData version". So I implemented a very hacky monkey patch to switch the version back to 4.01 at runtime.

Sure enough, it is only the import that does not work - once you can get it to be recognized by Build Work Zone it doesn't matter what version it is, the app runs fine.

I'm not planning on really using this in the app, I just wanted to verify that it is only the deployment that fails. In the end my solution was not so interesting, I just reverted the backend service to use 4.0. I hope the Build Work Zone team will address this quickly since there must be a lot of users with apps using 4.01 now who are experiencing issues with Build Work Zone thanks to the generator.

P.S. It is not only Build Work Zone that does not support version 4.01 - the UI5 Language Assistant also seems to have an issue. When my localService folder's metadata.xml has version 4.01 set, all the XML views lose their code completion in VS Code. Setting it back to 4.0 fixes the issue.

entry.title: From Spreadsheets to Strategic Planning: Smarter Program Budgeting Starts with SAC
entry.content:
For years, Marketing Operations at SAP faced the same challenges every year: navigating a fast-paced budgeting cycle using Excel spreadsheets. Data lived in silos, collaboration was difficult, and leadership lacked real-time visibility into how investment decisions would impact the business. Despite the team’s best efforts, the process wasn’t good enough for the speed and precision marketing requires these days. 

A Clear Need for Transformation 

The marketing budgeting process needs a single source of truth- one centralized place where marketing and financial data can come together. Offline spreadsheets have become very complex, are slow to update, not easy to interpret and can have errors due to manual updates. Teams need transparency and a tool that eliminates manual effort to consolidate datasets. 

Most importantly, there is a need to make quick decisions during planning calls, not hours or days later after analysing the spreadsheets 

Building a Smarter, Faster Planning Environment with SAC 

Leveraging the existing SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC), Marketing Operations and CPIT teams joined to design a fully integrated planning module built directly in SAC. 

Using learnings from previous planning cycles, the team recreated the functionality of legacy spreadsheets—then enhanced it using SAC’s capabilities: 

  • Live planning data from core systems, all centralized inside SAC 
  • Data actions, fluid data entry, and currency conversion  
  • Multiple versions to model different scenarios and compare changes  
  • Automated master data sourcing, reducing maintenance and ensuring consistency 
  • A dashboard layer that visualizes changes and tells the story behind the numbers 
  • A data architecture prepared for downstream integrations into CRM and ISP 

The result is a collaborative, real-time planning experience, removing the need to email spreadsheets back and forth or wait for someone to “update the spreadsheet.” 

The Results: Real-Time Planning With Real Impact 

The impact of moving to SAC Planning was immediate: 

  • 94% faster calculations compared to offline spreadsheets 
  • Instant scenario modeling with real-time feedback for leadership 
  • Over 41 marketing attributes blended with financial data for deeper insights 
  • ~480 parameters simplified into three strategic investment levers 
  • Automated budget cascades across 10 organizations, five solution areas, and 8 Marketing Mix types 
  • 100% adoption of SAP running SAP, improving transparency and trust 
  • Elimination of manual imports into visualization tools 

What previously required hours of manual adjustments and spreadsheet now happens in minutes. Leaders can explore scenarios live, evaluate impacts, and align decisions without waiting. 

As M&S CCO Tom Lavin put it: 

“We used our technology to streamline the modeling efforts and make key decisions in real time.” 

This wasn’t just process improvement—it was a full transformation of how Marketing plans investments and responds to change. 

Laying the Foundation for the Future 

With SAC Planning in place, Marketing now has the agility and governance needed to continuously evolve. The new tool brings clarity, consistency, and confidence to planning decisions, setting the stage for broader SAC adoption across the entire Marketing Program Budget Planning process. 

When we start using SAP technology the way it’s meant to be used, we unlock not just efficiency—but smarter, more strategic decision-making.

entry.title: Re: Credit management related issues - update group chang...
entry.content:
Dear Blake45,

First, I’d like to confirm that reassigning the  update group from 000012 to 000015 was the correct approach for this situation.

Regarding your questions:

1) Removing the reassigned SO value after the change of the update group:

To solve this problem, you have to rebuild the commitments after changing the update group. You can do this in the Rebuild Credit Management Data (F4234) app. Here's a link to the documentation of the job template: Rebuild Credit Exposure

2) Error after releasing the documented credit decision

For this type of issue, please open a customer incident so our support team can investigate further.

Best wishes
Christina

entry.title: Re: From Legacy ERP to SAP S/4HANA: Why Quality Governance is the Secret Sauce
entry.content: Spot on, @Corrie ! 'Get it clean, then keep it clean' is the ultimate golden rule. In my experience with complex S/4HANA migrations, technical success is often hollow if data integrity isn't governed from day one. entry.title: Re: MuleSoft vs SAP Integration Suite: Which One Fits Your Enterprise?
entry.content:
SAP CPI does not have currently a solution for individual message processing for Event Mesh messages. Which is a huge miss! ( Only for JMS, but the User Experience for that JMS is very low.  )

SAP CPI does not have a built-in resend functionality, what I assume MuleSoft has.  CPI does not persist the messages only with additional development effort.  This makes CPI a half-ready product in my opinion, because the error handling is just simply not there yet.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Production Planning and Manufacturing Certification – course overview and 2026 dates
entry.content:
To make your path to certification clear and structured, we’ve organized the learning journey into three parts: an optional basics overview, mandatory modules for certification preparation, and an additional deep-dive course. We’re also offering bundled tracks with fixed dates.

Basics overview (optional, for beginners)

  • S42010 – Exploring Business Processes in SAP S/4HANA Production Planning
  • S42020 – Exploring Business Processes in SAP S/4HANA Discrete Shopfloor Control
  • S42025 – Exploring Business Processes in SAP S/4HANA Process Shopfloor Control

Mandatory trainings for certification preparation

  • S4210 – Basic Data for Manufacturing and Product Management in SAP S/4HANA
  • S4222 – Production Planning in SAP S/4HANA – Fiori-based
  • S4225 – SAP S/4HANA Production Orders

Additional recommended training (deep dive)

  • S4224 – Production Planning with SAP S/4HANA PP/DS

Recommended learning sequence

  • Optional foundation: S42010/S42020/S42025 (overview)
  • Mandatory modules in this order: S4210 → S4222 → S4225
  • Optional afterwards: S4224 (PP/DS) for advanced planning

Tracks and dates 2026

  • Track 1 (May/June 2026)

    • S4210: May 18–22, 2026 (Walldorf or SAP Live Class, SLC)
    • S4222: May 27–29, 2026 (SAP Live Class, SLC)
    • S4225: June 15–19, 2026 (Walldorf or SAP Live Class, SLC)

  • Track 2 (September 2026)

    • S4210: September 7–11, 2026 (Walldorf or SAP Live Class, SLC)
    • S4222: September 14–16, 2026 (SAP Live Class, SLC)
    • S4225: October 05–09, 2026 (Hamburg or SAP Live Class, SLC)

  • Track 3 (December 2026)

    • S4210: November 30–December 4, 2026 (Walldorf or SAP Live Class, SLC)
    • S4225: December 7–11, 2026 (Walldorf or SAP Live Class, SLC)
    • S4222: December 14–16, 2026 (SAP Live Class, SLC)

Notes for exam preparation

The three mandatory modules cover the core topics assessed in the SAP S/4HANA Production Planning and Manufacturing certification. Plan the exam after completing all mandatory modules and allow 1–2 weeks for review and hands-on practice. The basics overview is optional but helps newcomers get up to speed with processes and terminology. Formats: on-site in Walldorf, Hamburg or virtual, as indicated above.

entry.title: Re: Absence Counting Rules 2.0 in Time Management - 2H 2023
entry.content:
Hi @PriyankaAgarwal 

First of all, great blog and thank you for sharing such valuable insights.

We have an extremely flexible workforce where employees frequently work based on demand. As a result, we are using Temporary Work Schedules for these scenarios. From your explanation, it is clear that we can either:

  1. Use FTE/Working Hours per Week, and/or
  2. Use Employee Time: Number of Hours in the Absence Counting Rule parameters.

We opted for the second approach, as it better fits our current landscape.

While this works as expected for single-day absences, we are experiencing issues with multi-day absences. Specifically, when an employee has reduced hours on certain days (which can vary frequently), those days should count as 0.5 instead of 1.

For example, if an employee requests a full week of absence covering five working days, and two of those days have reduced hours, the system should count 4 days instead of 5.

Could you please advise which approach you would recommend in this scenario?

Additionally, I understand that there were plans to include Work Schedule as a parameter in the Absence Counting Rule. Is this enhancement already planned or available in the roadmap?

Thank you once again for your support.

Cheers,
Pedro

entry.title: Re: Issue with Bank Statement Processing Rules for Bank Fees
entry.content:
Dear @Claudia234,

In Public Cloud using Memo Line as a condition in bank statement processing rules is supported so the rule design itself is valid. However, SAP documents that interpretation algorithm 001 (Standard Algorithm) processes only alphanumeric characters and does not handle special-character scenarios. Based on this documented standard limitation, automatic posting should not be considered reliable when the memo line contains non-alphanumeric or special characters, including cases where the memo line contains Chinese text even if the same rule works for Latin text such as BANK CHARGES

The SAP-supported approach is to avoid relying on standard algorithm 001 for this case and instead use processing instruction 011 which SAP explicitly states can be used in Reprocess Bank Statement Items or through processing rules

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3555197

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/4865ed7d26304aa1b011db7a52131bea.html

In addition, it is important to verify that the Chinese text is imported into SAP exactly as received from the bank. SAP also documents that if the bank statement uses a structured memo line in field :86:, the correct format handling is required, for example MT940_STRUCTURED instead of the standard MT940 mapping

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/5481c611480e4c23bf49e3af651d8e4d.html

Summary:

The issue is most likely a standard interpretation limitation, not a problem with the posting rule target account. For memo text containing non-alphanumeric or special characters, including Chinese text, the recommended SAP-supported solution is to first verify that the text is imported into SAP as expected and that the correct statement format is used and then use processing instruction 011 instead of relying on standard memo-line matching with algorithm 001

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: SAC Live Connection to S/4HANA Private Cloud via CORS
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community, I'm currently connecting SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) to SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition using the CORS Direct method for a Live Data Connection. I'm running into two issues that I haven't been able to fully resolve, and I'd appreciate any guidance from the community.

My current setup

  • SAP Analytics Cloud 
  • SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition 
  • Live Data Connection configured using CORS Direct method
  • Authentication method: User Name and Password (Basic Auth)

Issue 1 - Credentials required every time I open a story

Every time I open a Story that uses a Live model connected to S/4HANA, I get prompted to enter my S/4HANA username and password before the data loads.

My current workaround: I open a Fiori launchpad session in a separate browser tab first so that the S/4HANA session cookie exists in the browser, and then the data loads in SAC without the popup.

This is not a sustainable solution for end users. Is this related to the SSL certificate on the S/4HANA side? Or is this purely a missing SSO configuration?

Issue 2 - SAC mobile app: stories load with no data (design only)

On the SAC mobile app (iOS/Android) (or the browser same issue ), I can log into SAC successfully, I can see my stories and the design renders correctly, but no data is loaded. The charts and tables are empty.

I tried opening a Fiori session in the mobile browser first (same workaround as desktop), but it doesn't help, data still doesn't appear in the app.

My understanding is that CORS relies on browser-level cookie sharing, which native mobile apps don't support in the same way. Is this correct? Is CORS fundamentally incompatible with the SAC mobile app/ browser for Live Connections?

 

SO my question for the community : 

- How eliminate the S/4HANA credential popup everytime ??

- Is there a recommended architecture that handles both desktop and mobile in a Private Cloud / On-Premise-like context?

entry.title: Re: ODBC failure on basic call
entry.content: I spoke with a SQL Anywhere ODBC driver developer to confirm what is happening.  The buffer is measured in bytes and SQL Anywhere expects the buffer to be an even value when processing wide characters i.e., SQL_C_WCHAR, and sized sufficiently for the value being accessed.  In the SQLTables example, it is using SQL_C_CHAR so is not impacted by this.  So, use an even value when you are setting the catalog[i].BufferLength with SQL_C_WCHAR.

entry.title: Interview Assessment Pending notification - SF RECRUITING
entry.content:
I created and enabled an email template to cover pending interview assessments.

Then enabled the recruiting mail trigger for the "Interview Assessments Outstanding" with this condition: "If an interview assessment form has been incomplete for 1 day following the interview date, send an email every 1 day. If no interview date is specified, this email does not trigger. Send a maximum of 3 reminder emails."

---

However, the notifications are not being sent.

After reading some SAP KBAs, I have done the following:

  • Checked that the provisioning job isn't failing. It runs completely.
  • Used reporting that the pending interview assessments are not more than 32,000 to ensure they are not stopping the mails from coming through.
  • Ensure that 'Enable Stack Ranker-style Interview Assessments' is ticked.

----

In addition, with the 1H2026 Release for the new applicant management experience, using the nudge button in the feedback pending column, the email notification triggers.

Kindly advise what else I can do for the email trigger to work as expected without clicking the nudge button.

Thank You!

entry.title: Does anybody have the chance to play with the sap note 2608955 - How to dynamically adjust partition
entry.content:
Dear all,

I found a note explaining how to create partitions dynamically in a Flowgraphs - SDI. Using this approach, I can create a custom program in the source system to adjust the partition definition through parameters instead of creating them manually.

Unfortunately, after following the instructions, no parameters are actually used during execution and all the data is handled in a single partition. If I specify the partition values manually, the data is correctly split into the corresponding partitions.

I am using the same code as propoused:

CREATE PROCEDURE "MY_SCHEMA"."public::VariableSupportForTaskPartitions_SP"(
IN MY_VAR_VAL1 INT,
IN MY_VAR_VAL2 INT,
IN MY_VAR_VAL3 INT,
IN MY_VAR_VAL4 INT)
LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT
SQL SECURITY INVOKER DEFAULT SCHEMA "MY_SCHEMA" AS

BEGIN

SET 'MY_VAR1' = :MY_VAR_VAL1;
SET 'MY_VAR2' = :MY_VAR_VAL2;
SET 'MY_VAR3' = :MY_VAR_VAL3;
SET 'MY_VAR4' = :MY_VAR_VAL4;

EXEC 'START TASK "MY_SCHEMA"."public::VariableSupportForTaskPartition"';

UNSET 'MY_VAR1';
UNSET 'MY_VAR2';
UNSET 'MY_VAR3';
UNSET 'MY_VAR4';

END;

The call in the source system, in this scenario BW is made using the next example of statement:

CALL MY_SCHEMA."public::VariableSupportForTaskPartitions_SP"(20,40,60,80);

Note that upon the note, the exec clause does not need the explicit sending of the values, all them are handle with the "environtment" variables.

It seems that the parameter values are being ignored, as they are not passed in the execution call. However, when I check the table _SYS_TASK.TASK_PARTITION_EXECUTIONS, the partitions are defined, but the partition values in the SET clause are not interpreted correctly or are not successfully passed in the call.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: SAP Commerce Cloud Q1 2026 Release Highlights
entry.content:
SAP Commerce Cloud Q1 2026 Release Highlights

The Q1 2026 release of SAP Commerce Cloud represents a significant step forward in our ongoing commitment to deliver intelligent, unified commerce solutions. This release brings expanded AI capabilities, modernized payment flexibility, and new monetization opportunities - enabling brands to innovate faster and deliver exceptional customer experiences.

At the center of this innovation is our effort to remove complexity across the commerce value chain. Whether optimizing payments, automating product content, or enabling subscription-driven business models, SAP Commerce Cloud continues to support enterprises in creating seamless digital journeys with agility
and scale.

Checkout.com Integration

The introduction of our dedicated integration marks a transformative upgrade to the payment landscape in SAP Commerce Cloud. Powered through the SAP Commerce Cloud, open payment framework, this integration delivers a high-performance payment experience enriched with AI-driven optimization. Intelligent Acceptance analyzes each transaction and dynamically chooses the best routing, exemption, and retry strategies to maximize approval rates. With deep global acquiring capabilities, advanced fraud tools, and real-time account updates, merchants gain a robust payment foundation that drives higher revenue and operational efficiency.

 


Beyond the Checkout.com integration, the Open Payment Framework itself has been enhanced to support broader payment needs across all PSPs. Merchants can now offer Buy Now Pay Later options through providers such as Klarna, enabling greater flexibility and higher conversion. Split payment support allows customers to combine gift cards and digital payment methods in a single transaction, removing checkout barriers and reducing drop-off. Additionally, the new PCI DSS 4 compliance ensures a strengthened security posture and regulatory readiness for global operations.

AI Media Attachments

This release also introduces AI Media Attachments, which dramatically increase creation. By extracting structured information from product manuals, spec sheets, and PDFs, the system generates enriched and accurate product descriptions automatically. This improves product data quality, enhances search relevance, and reduces manual workload for merchandising teams.

SAP Subscription & Billing for Composable Storefront

Finally, we are expanding support for subscription-based business models through native SAP Subscription & Billing integration for Composable Storefront. With a new API and routing mechanism acting as a service provider interface, brands can now implement recurring revenue offerings more easily, leveraging headless storefront capabilities with reduced integration complexity and faster time-to-value.

 

The Q1 2026 release underscores SAP’s commitment to delivering a commerce platform that is flexible, intelligent, and built for long-term growth. By harmonizing advanced AI, modernized payments, and scalable architecture, SAP Commerce Cloud empowers businesses to stay ahead in an increasingly dynamic digital world.

 

For more details on the above release features, check out the following resources: 

  • SAP Commerce Cloud Release Navigator single access point to release content 
  • Watch the replay of the Q1 2026 SAP Commerce Cloud Release Recap webcast 
  • Sign up for Q1 2026 Tech-Focused Update webcast

SAP Help Portal 

  • SAP Commerce Cloud What’s New 

 

Please share your feedback in a comment and follow our SAP Commerce Cloud topic page to connect with the community!

entry.title: Foreign Exchange Adjustments for Cash G/L Accounts (Mixed Currencies)
entry.content:
Hello SAP Team,

We have the below request from the business. They would like to inquire about SAP process options to record unrealized gain/loss in SAP for cash G/L account balances as of the month‑end reporting date.

  • They have several accounts that are functionally different from the company code/functional currency of the legal entity (for example, for a UK company code where the company code currency is GBP, the balance is in USD).
  • They are not using the standard Foreign Currency Valuation app/process, as some G/L accounts have a mix of currencies, some of which do not represent “true” G/L balances (posted and cleared in different currencies).
  • Profit centers are often not reconciled to reduce unnecessary postings.
  • They are currently using the Verify Currency Adjustment app to record manual FX adjustments. They would like to know if there is an alternate, more systematic way to record these month‑end adjustments.

If anyone has experienced a similar scenario, could you please share your feedback or recommendations?

Thanks,
Priya

entry.title: Re: How to remove the negative sign on deduction wagetypes
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Have you ever noticed certain custom wage types showing a ( - ) sign in the payslip? 🤔

Many assume it’s a payroll calculation issue — but in most cases, it’s actually a configuration setting in PE51.

🔍 What’s the Reason?

The negative sign is controlled by payslip form configuration (Conversion Rule & Column Type), not by payroll logic.

✅ Quick Fix

✔ Go to PE51 → Select Country Grouping & Form Name
✔ Maintain Line Layout and assign proper Column Type (e.g., “R”)
✔ Use BETRG field with Conversion Rule 43
✔ Update Group 2 in Window (W2) for required wage types

🎯 Key Takeaway

👉 It’s a display issue, not a calculation error
👉 Small configuration changes can fix the payslip instantly

📩 Need a step-by-step document with screenshots?
Feel free to DM me — happy to help!

#SAP #SAPHCM #Payroll #HRTech #SAPConsultant #HCM #ERP #PayrollManagement

entry.title: Re: AI Developer Challenge - Week 1: Grounding with SAP Generative AI Hub
entry.content:
"Die Tafel" in Germany refers to a nationwide movement and its umbrella organization, Tafel Deutschland e.V. Here's a breakdown based on the provided context: * **What it is:** Tafel Deutschland e.V. is the umbrella organization for over 970 local " Tafeln" (food banks) across Germany. It is a non-profit association where 13 state associations and local Tafeln are members. These local Tafeln and state associations are independently organized as associations themselves. * **Its Mission/Activities:** Tafel Deutschland supports the local Tafeln organizationally, professionally, and log istically. It represents the Tafeln to politics, business, and society, ensures quality standards, provides further training, and helps with structural development. It also coordinates nationwide large-scale food donations, offers advice for new Tafel establishments, and raises public awareness about poverty and food waste. * **Who it helps:** The local Tafeln primarily support people with low incomes, including the unemployed, retirees, single parents, students, and refugees. Approximately 29% of those supported are children and young people, and about 20% are seniors. Tafeln also supply social institutions like women's shelters and homeless shelters. * **Role of Volunteers:** Volunteers are the backbone of the Tafel movement. Around 77,000 active individuals, 94% of whom are volunteers, are involved. Their commitment is essential for the work of the Tafeln. * **History:** The first Tafel in Germany was founded in Berlin in 199 3 by Sabine Werth and the "Initiativgruppe Berliner Frauen e.V." The umbrella organization, Tafel Deutschland, was established in 1995.

id 

GE306430

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite, enhanced edition - The “Trust Layer” for Agentic AI
entry.content:
For the last year, the world has been captivated by AI’s ability to answer. The next era belongs to AI that can act.

Agentic AI promises something bigger than copilots and chat interfaces: a digital workforce that can sense what’s happening, reason over context, and initiate the next best action across finance, supply chain, HR, procurement, and customer operations. But here’s the reality most enterprises discover quickly: the leap from clever prototype to production-grade agent isn’t a model problem — it’s a trust problem.

Trust is earned in the connective tissue of the enterprise: identity, governance, reliable integration, observability, and controlled execution across SAP and non‑SAP systems. That’s exactly why we’re excited to announce SAP Integration Suite, enhanced edition — designed to help organizations move from AI experimentation to agentic outcomes by providing an integration foundation where agents can operate safely, securely, and at scale.

From “AI that knows” to “AI that does”: why integration becomes strategic

Agentic AI doesn’t live in a single application. It lives between applications — where approvals happen, where exceptions are resolved, where events trigger downstream actions, where APIs enforce guardrails, and where process continuity must be protected.

This is the moment when integration becomes more than plumbing. It becomes the execution fabric for AI.

SAP’s vision for Integration Suite is explicit: unify your enterprise with secure, AI‑ready integration and the governance required to scale agentic AI across the business — not as an add-on, but as an enterprise capability.

What’s new in enhanced edition — and why it’s built for agentic reality

SAP Integration Suite, enhanced edition provides access to newly released AI capabilities that focus on two things agentic AI needs most:

1) Speed to value: AI that accelerates how integrations are built

Enhanced edition includes AI features aimed at boosting developer productivity, including:

  • Text‑to‑Flow generation — build integration flows using a natural language interface.
  • Script Optimization — analyze, refactor, and optimize Groovy or JavaScript scripts inside iFlows.

In an agentic world, integration teams must iterate faster — because agents will continuously evolve how they use tools, call APIs, and respond to events. These capabilities are about compressing the distance between an idea and a governed, deployable integration asset.

2) Operational trust: AI that helps integrations run with confidence

Agents introduce new scale and new patterns — and that means you need sharper operational insight. Enhanced edition includes AI-driven operations efficiency features such as:

  • API Anomaly Detection — AI-driven observability that detects unusual behavior and flags issues early.
  • API Traffic Prediction — identify trends and predict upcoming API call volumes to support smarter planning and load management.

This is what makes the positioning “agentic enabler” real: it’s not just that AI helps you build integrations — it helps you run the execution fabric with the resilience and foresight required for autonomous action.

More than AI features: the full suite that agents can safely act through

Enhanced edition also includes the broader platform capabilities needed to operationalize agentic scenarios end-to-end, including:

  • The full suite of integration capabilities (application integration, B2B integration, API management, edge integration) and broad connectivity.
  • Integration Suite, advanced event mesh for event-driven architecture use cases.
  • SAP Document AI, plus Alert Notification Service and Transport Management Service to support intelligent automation patterns and governed lifecycle management.

In other words: enhanced edition is designed to be a practical on‑ramp to a future where AI agents don’t just inform decisions — they execute decisions across the enterprise, under control.

How customers can move to enhanced edition (designed to be seamless)

Many customers already run SAP Integration Suite today — and moving to enhanced edition is intended to be an in-place plan upgrade rather than a disruptive reimplementation.

We provide documented upgrade guidance for updating an existing tenant to a higher service plan (with the note that it’s a commercial metadata update and is irreversible once applied). See official documentation on help.sap.com.
Additionally, our community guidance on tenant upgrades has been updated to include upgrade paths to the newly released enhanced_edition. See SAP Note 2903776.

For customers modernizing from older integration platforms, SAP also provides structured guidance and tools to support transition journeys to SAP Integration Suite. See migration factory page on sap.com.

Commercial options: choose the model that matches your AI journey

Agentic adoption is rarely linear — which is why commercial flexibility matters.

SAP BTP supports two commercial models:

  • Subscription-based: fixed cost for subscribed services.
  • Consumption-based: flexibility to use eligible services as business needs evolve, available via models such as SAP BTP Enterprise Agreement / Cloud Platform Enterprise Agreement and Pay‑As‑You‑Go for SAP BTP.

And if your organization wants a low-friction entry point, choose Integration Suite free tier and a path to upgrade to a paid service plan.

You can find out more details on the different commercial models on SAP Discovery Centre.

A note on AI feature availability and rollout

The availability of AI features depend on the availability of SAP AI Core and the specific LLM model and corresponding version on that data centre. If you notice that AI features are not available in the DC of your choice, please get in touch with us via the comments section regarding your requirement.

The bigger picture: building an enterprise where AI can safely act

The most important shift isn’t “AI inside an app.” It’s AI as a participant in end-to-end business execution.

SAP Integration Suite, enhanced edition is built for that shift — providing the integration fabric where autonomous agents can access governed tools, respond to events, operate across hybrid landscapes, and execute actions with observability and control.

If your organization is serious about moving from AI inspiration to agentic transformation, the most strategic question isn’t “Which model should we use?” It is: Do we have an integration foundation strong enough for AI to act — at enterprise scale?

Frequently Asked Questions on Enhanced Edition

What are the commercial transition options to Enhanced Edition?

If you are a consumption-based (BTPEA/CPEA) Integration Suite customer, you already have access to Enhanced Edition. Just upgrade your current tenants to Enhanced Edition via our seamless upgrade process. If you are a subscription customer, please get in touch with your customer success partner or account executive to understand the best path to obtain Enhanced Edition entitlements.

If you are a customer using one of our older standalone service licenses, please reach out to us via your customer success partner or account executive as well for more details on how to obtain Enhanced Edition entitlements.

What is the technical upgrade/downgrade options to/from Enhanced Edition?

Technically, we support a seamless upgrade from any of the current Integration Suite plan to the Enhanced Edition. More details here: upgrade process & upgrade paths

We do not support downgrading Enhanced Edition tenants to any of the other Integration Suite editions.

Can we have a mix of Standard/Premium tenant with Enhanced Edition in the same landscape?

We do not recommend this since it is critical to have feature parity across the different landscape tiers.

I don’t have AI units; can I still use AI features in Enhanced Edition?

Yes. Currently, we are offering AI features as a promotion. Soon, we will start charging the AI features via AI units. Please stay tuned to this space as more details will follow in the next weeks on this topic.

What happens if I reach the fair usage limit for AI features in Enhanced Edition?

You will have to wait for the next calendar month to begin when your usage quota gets reset. This is a limitation only till we launch metering AI feature usage via AI units, after which you can buy more quota and use AI features based on your requirement.

Is MCP already available on Enhanced Edition if I am a beta customer?

Not yet; it will be launched soon. Please refer to our roadmap for the updated timelines.

How can I purchase additional quota for the included services like Document AI, TMS, ANS, etc. in Enhanced Edition?

If you are on a consumption-based (BTPEA/CPEA) license or a hybrid (subscription+consumption) license, you already have access to the services via your credits and can use them, once you it the limits in Enhanced Edition.

If you are a subscription-only customer, please get in touch with your customer success manager or account executive to buy more quota.

Can I upgrade the AEM broker to a larger size? What happens to the existing entitlement if I upgrade?

Yes, the broker can be upgraded to a bigger size. The entitlement will continue to remain, and you can provision another standard-100 broker if you wish.

Is there a dedicated SKU for a non-production tenant of Enhanced Edition?

No. We only have one SKU for Enhanced Edition and it can be used in both production and non-production modes.

Is there a dedicated TDD SKU for Enhanced Edition?

This is currently work in progress and will be launched in the near future. Please stay tuned to this space for more information.

entry.title: How to Copy Price From PO to Intercompany Sales Order

entry.title: SAP Developer News for April 16th, 2026
entry.content:
ITEMS

UX Innovation Day in Silicon Valley on 11 June 

  • Event page https://events.sap.com/us-ux-innovation-day-siliconvalley-2026/en_us/home.html  

SAP BTP ABAP Environment – Pre-Upgrade Option for Release 2605 

  • Blog post https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-btp-abap-environment-pre-upgrade-option-for-release-2605/ba-p/14347147  

XML Annotations: The Essentials Before Adapting Documentation Samples to Your Project 

  • Mio’s blog post https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/xml-annotations-the-essentials-before-adapting-documentation-samples-to/ba-p/14369241 
  • OData Deep Dive mission https://developers.sap.com/mission.odata-deep-dive.html  
  • OData Deep Dive rewrite blog post https://qmacro.org/blog/posts/2026/02/02/odata-deep-dive-rewrite-in-the-open/  

Edge Integration Cell APIs and Cloud Integration AI-assisted error resolution 

  • Edge Integration Cell APIs: https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/local-odata-api-access-for-edge-integration-cell?locale=en-US  
  • AI-assisted error resolution: https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/analyze-message-processing-errors-with-ai-f27f95d7b90b4f13aea22b399418b702?locale=en-US  

DYK #12 AI Launchpad Leaderboard 

  • The Model Library topic in the SAP Help Portal https://help.sap.com/docs/ai-launchpad/sap-ai-launchpad/model-library  

CHAPTER TITLES

00:00 Intro 

00:07 UX Innovation Day in Silicon Valley on 11 June 

01:00 SAP BTP ABAP Environment – Pre-Upgrade Option for Release 2605 

02:00 XML Annotations: The Essentials Before Adapting Documentation Samples to Your Project 

03:16 Edge Integration Cell APIs and Cloud Integration AI-assisted error resolution 

04:25 DYK #12 AI Launchpad Leaderboard 

TRANSCRIPT

[INTRO]
This is the SAP Developer News for April the 16th, 2026.

[DJ]
If you're in the Silicon Valley area in June and are interested in the intersection between AI and UX, then there's an event just for you. It's on the 11th of June at the SAP AppHaus in Palo Alto, and it's an in-person event only. It's for developers, architects, and technical consultants, and it's all about the AI functionality being built into the SAP Fiori elements framework. There's AI for business users and AI for application developers. The event page has an agenda for the day, which is currently tentative, but it gives us a good idea of the format and content. There's also a link on there for registration. Anyway, check it out. Link in the description.

[Mamikee]
We have an important update for SAP BTP ABAP environment users. We're offering a free pre-upgrade option for release 2605, giving customers and partners the chance to test their existing applications ahead of the standard upgrade, which is planned for May 16th and 17th. Now some of the key milestones have already passed. Planned new features were announced on April April 13th, and pre-upgraded systems are scheduled for upgrade on April 17th, which means teams already participating should now be focused on regression testing and validating their custom-built apps. Now, looking ahead, Hot Fix Collection 2 is planned for April 30th, and any issues found during testing should be reported by May 1st using component BC-CP-ABA. Now, the goal is simple. help make the upgrade a no-event and avoid day one surprises. You can read more in the link below.

[DJ]
Fiori elements based UIs are great, but sometimes it's hard for us to grok how they can be controlled and configured, which is mostly through annotations, which are organized in vocabularies, expressed in metadata, which is usually in the form of XML. So any help is welcome. And in that context, we wanted to point you to a great blog post by SAP community member Mio Yasutake, who takes time to explain these topics in great detail, how things fit together based upon samples and documentation for UI5 and in the context of CAP and RAP-based services. There's lots of detail and it's well worth a read. Thanks Mio! Also if you want more detail to complement this then we have the OData deep dive mission over on the tutorial navigator and in there there are specific tutorials on metadata vocabularies and annotations ready to go as well. Happy learning!

[Antonio]
Hola, SAP Developers! Two quick updates for Edge Integration Cell and Cloud Integration users. First, the local OData APIs for Edge Integration Cell just got better. You can now authenticate using Client ID and Client Secret. More secure, easy to automate. Now, a quick reminder that there are many APIs in Edge Integration Cell that you can use. Monitoring APIs, for example, to track message processing. and management APIs for message stores, data stores, JMS brokers, and variables. These are perfect for building custom dashboards or for automating operations or integrating with your existing monitoring tools. Now, in Cloud Integration, we have AI-assisted error resolution. When your integration flow fails, you can now use AI to analyze the error, get root cause suggestions, and even get remediation steps. So it's like having an integration buddy next to you. which can save time debugging and gets you back on track faster. So check the links in the description for full documentation on both features. ¡Hasta luego!

[Nora]
Did you know that you can compare foundation models right in AI Launchpad? So just jump over to Generative AI Hub in your AI Launchpad and open the model library. And then here you can click on Leaderboard. And now you see all the models that we have available here. and over here you can see the different scores so the HELM score for example tells you how well the model performed in a multiple choice exam across a variety of topics the chatbot arena score tells you how well the model performed in a head-to -head against different AI models and then the AIRBench refusal rate and the AIRBench discrimination bias refusal rate tells you how well the model is handling harmful or biased requests and then all the way over here you have the output and input token cost and the context window size. Now this really helped me to find the right model for my use case depending on cost and context window size and performance overall. I honestly usually don't go to the leaderboard, I head directly to the chart because here you can very easily compare the models.

In my plan, I am using the assignment fields with a formula [if(assigmentproratio>50,1,0)], this allows me to identify that one assignment is greater than another, but it does not solve my question of which business unit has that assignment. Has anyone done something like this?

entry.title: Sorting Issue in Billing Document Item Details CDS Hierarchy
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Currently We are experiencing an issue with sorting of Billing Document Item which were associated with Invoice List documents in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

Business Requirement:

To display Invoice List data in a structured hierarchy in the PDF Document developed on BTP.

  • Header Level: Invoice List
  • Item Level: Invoice List Items
  • Sub-Item Level: Billing Documents associated with each Invoice List item

The expectation is that Billing Document Items should be displayed in a consistent and sorted order (e.g., by Billing Document and Item Number in ascending order).

Current Behavior:

The Billing Document Item details (3rd level CDS) are not appearing in a consistent or sorted order. The sequence appears random or inconsistent across executions.

Technical Details:

  • We are using only standard CDS views 
  • CDS hierarchy:
    1. Invoice List (Header)
    2. Invoice List Item (Level 2)
    3. Billing Document Items (Level 3)</

entry.title: During the Hiring Process
entry.content:
It’s time for the hiring process! We have already reviewed a few measures you can take to detect potential application fraud during the candidate selection and before the interview. Now, let’s see how SAP Fieldglass features can help you during your hiring process. 

Work Order Approval: Segregation of Duties 

During your Work Order Approval workflow, you can include additional steps to enhance security during your hiring process, such as multiple approvals, comments, and ratings. 

These are some benefits of taking these actions: 

  • Requiring approvals from multiple parties reduces the risk that a single individual approves fraudulent or incorrect work orders. 
  • Mandating comments forces approvers to actively engage with the work order, consider its details, and justify their decisions, making it less likely that approvals are given without careful consideration. 
  • Providing ratings offers a quick, quantifiable assessment of the work order's quality, necessity, and compliance, helping to flag potentially risky or non-compliant work orders for further review. 
  • Requiring documented feedback (comments and ratings) from approvers creates an audit trail that promotes accountability.  

By incorporating these elements into your Work Order Approval workflow, you improve the security and integrity of your hiring process in SAP Fieldglass. 

Interview Upload 

Another feature that can help you detect potential fraud during a selection process is the Interview Upload. 

The Interview Upload feature helps you to verify the candidate's identity by recording interview details linked to specific candidates. This creates an audit trail that matches uploaded interviews to applicant profiles. The system can detect multiple interviews for the same position submitted under different identities and flag suspicious names or ID mismatches that might indicate fraudulent submissions. 

Also, it helps prevent submission of pre-recorded or fabricated interviews, enabling verification of interview dates against application timelines. This documentation helps confirm that the interviews are genuine and submitted at the appropriate time in the hiring process. 

Uploaded interviews serve as permanent records that can be reviewed at any time you need. This transparency ensures that hiring decisions are based on verifiable, legitimate information. 

Using the Interview Upload feature, you can create a more secure, transparent, and efficient hiring process.

Job Seeker Review 

The Job Seeker Review tool is designed to simplify the evaluation and selection process for job seekers who have applied to job postings. It can help you detect candidate fraud during the hiring process. 

Using AI, the Job Seeker Review can validate skills, identify inconsistencies, and flag potential risks in submitted resumes and profiles. It helps you identify discrepancies between a candidate's claimed experience and the job requirements. 

You can also rate job seekers, compare and score them. 

SAP Fieldglass's Job Seeker Review combines machine learning, identity verification, and historical tracking to build a comprehensive fraud-detection system. You can reduce risk, save time and resources, and ultimately hire candidates you can trust.  

Smart verification isn't just efficient, it's essential in today's hiring processes.  

Now that you know some features that can help you during your hiring process, let’s see next how SAP Fieldglass features can help you during the offer and start stages. Don’t miss it! 

 

 

For the purposes of this guide, “candidate fraud” means deliberate misrepresentation of identity, credentials, work history, or qualifications during recruitment. This guide is preventive and educational; it does not make legal determinations.

Hi SAP Experts

If a customer wants to order a part to perform a self-repair on a finished product that is in warranty, how should we handle such a scenario in SAP?

Currently, we are using the master warranty as a (warranty) BOM to display warranty coverage and parts that can be sold separately later to the customer. Now, while using the standard SAP warranty module and executing action A200 for warranty check, we are getting an ABAP dump. Need guidance on how we should set up our master data correctly so we do not get this dump.

entry.title: 1H 2026 SuccessFactors Onboarding Enhancement: Predelivered Custom Task Template Objects
entry.content:
Managing custom tasks in your onboarding and offboarding processes just got significantly easier. The 1H 2026 release introduces a game-changing enhancement: predelivered custom task template objects that eliminate the manual setup burden and accelerate your time to value.

The Challenge: Manual Configuration Overhead

Previously, when organizations wanted to capture custom data for their onboarding or offboarding tasks, they had to manually create custom MDF (Master Data Framework) objects for each custom task. This process was:

- Time-consuming – Admins spent considerable time designing and creating MDF objects from scratch

- Error-prone – Manual configuration increased the risk of setup mistakes

- Complex – Required deep technical knowledge of MDF object structure and configuration

- Repetitive – Similar custom task requirements across organizations meant duplicating the same effort

 

The Solution: Ready-to-Use Template Objects

We're excited to introduce an increase of the Onboarding-specific MDF limit up to 500, as well as the concept of predelivered custom task template objects that are now available out-of-the-box in SuccessFactors Onboarding. These templates provide ready-made MDF objects specifically designed for custom tasks, allowing you to:

 

Get Started Faster

No more starting from scratch. Select from a library of predelivered template objects that are immediately available in your system. Simply choose the template that matches your needs and configure your custom task in minutes, not hours.

 

Reduce Configuration Errors

Each template object is pre-configured with the optimal structure for custom task data collection, including:

- Proper field definitions and data types

- Correct security settings (RBP-secured with subject user field)

- Built-in searchability for key fields (activity ID, process ID, subject user)

- Appropriate permission categories

- The following field types are supported:

  • Boolean

  • Date

  • DateTime

  • Decimal

  • Generic Object

  • Number

  • String

  • Time

  • User


The following field character limits apply:

Custom FieldCharacter Limit

String + Users

40

Generic Object + Number + Boolean

20

Date + Time + DateTime

10

Decimal

10


Simplified Administration

The predelivered templates eliminate the complexity of MDF object creation. Administrators can now focus on defining the business logic of their custom tasks rather than wrestling with technical configuration details.

 

Flexibility for Both Onboarding and Offboarding

All template objects support both onboarding and offboarding processes, giving you maximum flexibility to use them across your entire employee lifecycle.

 

How It Works

The predelivered custom task template objects are automatically available in your 1H 2026 environment through a feature toggle that's enabled by default. When creating a custom task definition, you can now:

  1. Select from predelivered templates – Browse the available template objects in the Custom Tasks settings
  2. Associate the template – Link the template object to your custom task definition
  3. Configure your task – Define task name, description, module, and other properties
  4. Start using immediately – The template handles all the underlying data structure

 

Each template object provides:

- Dedicated database tables for optimal performance

- Reportable data through the Onboarding2 schema

- API support for integration scenarios

- Full localization support for global deployments

 

Seamless Integration with Existing Workflows

These predelivered templates work seamlessly with all existing custom task functionality, including:

- Custom task assignment and delegation

- Task completion workflows

- Notification templates

- Reporting and analytics

- REST APIs for custom task operations

 

Getting Started

For detailed information on creating and configuring custom tasks with the new predelivered template objects, please refer to the SAP Help documentation: Creating Custom MDF Object for Custom Tasks

 

Looking Ahead

This enhancement represents our commitment to simplifying administration while maintaining the flexibility and power that SuccessFactors Onboarding customers depend on. By providing predelivered templates, we're reducing the barrier to entry for custom tasks and enabling more organizations to take advantage of this powerful capability.

We're excited to see how you use these new templates to streamline your onboarding and offboarding processes!

 

---

 

Disclaimer:

This presentation/post, or any related document and SAP's strategy and possible future developments, products and or platforms directions and functionality are all subject to change and may be changed by SAP at any time for any reason without notice. The information in this presentation/post is not a commitment, promise or legal obligation to deliver any material, code or functionality. entry.title: Re: ODBC failure on basic call
entry.content:
// Instead of writing this ...
catalog[i].BufferLength = ( bufferSize + 1 );
// write this ...
// Note that some ODBC drivers have a 2-byte SQLWCHAR (like us) and 
// some ODBC drivers have a 4-byte SQLWCHAR, so write this.
catalog[i].BufferLength = ( characterSize + 1 ) * sizeof(SQLWCHAR);
// Example: for CHAR(10) data, characterSize = 10 and 
// the + 1 accounts for a null terminator.
// Analogously, the following works for non-wide SQLCHAR.
catalog[i].BufferLength = ( characterSize + 1 ) * sizeof(SQLCHAR);
// To generalize the "BufferLength" value ...
characterSize = n; // Ex: 10 for SQL CHAR(10) or VARCHAR(10)
sizeofCharacter = sizeof(SQLWCHAR); //or sizeof(SQLCHAR)
catalog[i].BufferLength = ( characterSize + 1 ) * sizeofCharacter;

entry.title: Re: Automate BTP Custom Domain certificates using Let's Encrypt
entry.content:
Hi @Ben ,

 

thanks for this awesome blog. I just found it as I am building a similar solution, renewing a certificate in the custom domain service within a gitlab pipeline.

I just came across the issue that the http challenge seems to be only working when already a valid certificate is deployed for the domain. During the initial setup this is not the case. Calling the /.well-known/... endpoint via http redirects to https and in that case the token is delivered but with an invalid certificate (CNAME still the cf....ondemand.com). Did you find any solution to use the let's encrypt stuff also for the initial verification? As said, as soon as a valid certificate is deployed once, renewing is not an issue.  But in the initial stage my script is just hanging at the point "Waiting for verification ..." with never coming to a response.

Or is this also the reason why you described in the git repository that the initial onboarding for a domain includes once the SSL setup manually?

 

Thanks for an update and cheers,

Michael

entry.title: A memorable SAP Community gathering at the SAP Inside Track Campinas 2026
entry.content:
The SAP Inside Track Campinas 2026 was truly a remarkable event, bringing together an impressive lineup of speakers and community members. It marked a special moment for the SAP ecosystem in Brazil, especially with the first visit of SAP Developer Advocates to the country. 

We had the honor of welcoming @noravonthenen @thomas_jung @RichHeilman @Vitaliy-R and SAP Community managers @antonia_zorn @josh_bentley, although Antonia is based in Brazil for some time, originally comes from Germany. Their presence, along with the broader SAP Community in Brazil, made this event in Campinas even more meaningful.

This visit was part of an extensive SAP CodeJam Roadshow across Brazil. For the first time, the country hosted more than 17 CodeJam sessions, hands-on training events focused on SAP development. It was an impressive milestone, and you can check the full events agenda in Brazil through this link (SIT + Codejam + SAP Meet ups).

The event itself took place at FACAMP, a beautiful university campus surrounded by green areas and open spaces. The day started with a warm welcome coffee break, followed by a full agenda that included lunch and additional networking breaks.

The opening session featured Josh and Antonia Zorn the SAP Community, followed by Thomas Jung and Rich, who spoke about ABAP AI topics that have been gaining strong traction in recent CodeJams. The agenda continued with several high-quality sessions from speakers like Vitaliy, Nora, @Secco , Leandro Dante, @MRobalinho among others. I’ve included links below so you can watch all sessions on YouTube.

 

Session Title Speaker(s)

ABAP AI — Artificial Intelligence in ABAP DevelopmentSAP Community managers @antonia_zorn @josh_bentley
SAP Developer Advocates @thomas_jung @RichHeilman
Tax Reform — 2026 RoadmapRoberto Paiva
GenAI — A Practical View of Generative AI in the SAP Ecosystem@noravonthenen
AI in SAP HANA Cloud@Vitaliy-R
Automatic PIX Implementation@MRobalinho 
AI, Geopolitics, and the IT Bubble — The SAP Architecture Market OutlookMarcelo Figueiredo Cabrera
From Data to Decisions — Accelerating Results with SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC)Renata Cristina
Transformation Focused on Business Value GenerationRodrigo Cordeiro
SAP BTP in Practice — Integrating Solutions and Connecting SAP ProductsDiego Yegros
SAP BTP Multitenancy in Practice — From Add-On to the FutureJoão Luiz Martins
GROW Retail Fashion — Architecture, Scalability, and Changes in S/4HANA Cloud (Public Edition)Diego Bortolucci
LLM, Joule, and RPT-1 — The New AI Stack for Structured Data in SAPLeandro Dante
AI for Functional Consultants — From Basic GPT to Prototyping@Secco 
Giveaways & Raffles — Closing SessionOrganization

One of the highlights of the event was its relaxed and friendly atmosphere. Attendees felt comfortable engaging with speakers, taking photos, and networking throughout the day. Unlike many events where people attend only a few sessions, most participants stayed until the very end, clear evidence of the quality and engagement of the content.

For me personally, it was also a special experience. I had the opportunity to contribute as a volunteer, supporting the live streaming of the event. While that meant I couldn’t take as many photos as I would have liked, it was incredibly rewarding to reconnect with friends and meet so many great professionals from the SAP community. 

Campinas continues to be a key hub for SAP events, attracting not only local participants but also many attendees from São Paulo. Alongside other cities like Ribeirão Preto and São José dos Campos, it plays an important role in strengthening the SAP ecosystem in the region.

 If you are an SAP professional, I strongly recommend keeping an eye on upcoming SAP Inside Track events in your area. Follow the SAP Community São Paulo group on LinkedIn and stay connected, you don’t want to miss these opportunities for learning, networking, and reconnecting with amazing people in the SAP world.

Special thanks to @RobertaFernande and the organizing team for making this event possible.

Feel free to share your own photos and experiences, or check out the SAP Inside Track LinkedIn page for more highlights from the event. The level of community engagement and social media activity around this event was truly outstanding.

It was a pleasure to be part of SAP Inside Track Campinas 2026. Looking forward to seeing you all again at future events.

entry.title: Re: Onboarding 1.0 Decommissioning Procedure & FAQ
entry.content:
@kenjodrey6 That's correct; the Standard Export Job should remember where it left off, and continue exporting from there.

@paul_bracy I'd refer you to the Onboarding 1.0 API protocols: https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-onboarding/overview-of-onboarding-1-0-and-offboarding-1-0-api/change-history?locale=en-US&version=LATEST

@evgeniia_kuzn answering your questions here but adding this to the FAQ:

  • Will SuperAdmin be available after May 14, 2026?
    • SuperAdmin will still be accessible after this time. It will be shut down and access removed between 1H 2026 and 1H 2027. However, accounts will be "locked" and normal operations will not be possible on them.
  • Is it a must to turn off all standard exports in SuperAdmin and clean up CPI/Boomi?
    • Once these jobs are all 'caught up' then yes, I'd recommend deactivating them and setting the various Features for an ONB1 tenant to inactive. This is not strictly required however.
  • Will old candidate's links to Onboarding Employee Portal and to Complete Paperwork (NES) still work after May 14th and until final purge?
    • These will no longer function.

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Suite, enhanced edition - The “Trust Layer” for Agentic AI
entry.content:
Hi Community,

"

1) Speed to value: AI that accelerates how integrations are built

Enhanced edition includes AI features aimed at boosting developer productivity, including:

  • Text‑to‑Flow generation — build integration flows using a natural language interface.
  • Script Optimization — analyze, refactor, and optimize Groovy or JavaScript scripts inside iFlows.

In an agentic world, integration teams must iterate faster — because agents will continuously evolve how they use tools, call APIs, and respond to events. These capabilities are about compressing the distance between an idea and a governed, deployable integration asset.

2) Operational trust: AI that helps integrations run with confidence

Agents introduce new scale and new patterns — and that means you need sharper operational insight. Enhanced edition includes AI-driven operations efficiency features such as:

  • API Anomaly Detection — AI-driven observability that detects unusual behavior and flags issues early.
  • API Traffic Prediction — identify trends and predict upcoming API call volumes to support smarter planning and load management.

This is what makes the positioning “agentic enabler” real: it’s not just that AI helps you build integrations — it helps you run the execution fabric with the resilience and foresight required for autonomous action.
